basic_details.job_title: ESIC Medical College & Hospital Chennai Senior Resident Recruitment 2026 – Walk in for 60 Posts
basic_details.organization_name: ESIC Medical College & Hospital, K.K. Nagar, Chennai – 600 078
basic_details.short_information: ESIC Medical College & Hospital, K.K. Nagar, Chennai has released an official advertisement for the engagement of 60 Senior Resident (3-Year Tenure) posts under the 7th Central Pay Commission (7th CPC) on a contractual basis. The recruitment is being conducted through a walk-in interview on July 8 and 9, 2026, across 23 medical departments. This is a significant opportunity for MD/MS/DNB/DM/MCh-qualified doctors seeking a structured, well-paid government hospital residency in Tamil Nadu's capital. Candidates who meet the eligibility criteria as per NMC-TEQ 2025 read with PGMER 2023 can appear

application_fee.details: The ESIC Senior Resident 2026 walk-in interview requires payment of a non-refundable application fee for most categories. Here is a clear breakdown. | Mode of Payment: Demand Draft only, drawn in favour of "ESI Fund Account No. 1", payable at Chennai, on any scheduled bank. The DD must be issued (i.e., after 17.06.2026). Cash, UPI, or online transfer are not accepted.

qualification.education_qualification: MD / MS / DNB / DM / MCh / M.Sc.+Ph.D.
qualification.details: Understanding the eligibility requirements is critical before appearing for the ESIC Senior Resident walk-in interview 2026. Below are the academic qualifications, age limits, and departmental preferences as per NMC-TEQ 2025 and PGMER 2023 norms. | Clinical specialties (broad specialties): Upper age limit - 45 years Pre-clinical / Para-clinical subjects (Anatomy, Biochemistry, Physiology, Pharmacology, Microbiology, Pathology, Forensic Medicine, Community Medicine): Upper age limit - 50 years | A candidate must hold one of the following: | MD / MS / DNB (Postgraduate degree in Doctor of Medicine, Master of Surgery, or Diplomate of National Board), or M.Sc. with Ph.D. in medical subjects - Anatomy, Biochemistry, Physiology, Pharmacology, or Microbiology | All candidates must satisfy eligibility as per NMC Teachers Eligibility Qualifications (TEQ) 2025 read with MSR/PGMER 2023 as on the date of interview. | This is a critical section for candidates - check your department's preferred order before appearing at the ESIC Chennai walk-in interview. | Two departments allow candidates from related specialties to also apply - this significantly widens eligibility for those departments: | Emergency Medicine: Candidates holding MD/MS/DNB in General Medicine, Anaesthesia, Respiratory Medicine, General Surgery, or Orthopaedics can appear - in addition to their own specialty. Physical Medicine & Rehabilitation: Candidates with MD (General Medicine) + Diploma in PMR, or MS/DNB in General Surgery or Orthopaedics are also eligible.

salary_details.details: One of the most sought-after aspects of ESIC Senior Resident recruitment is its competitive pay structure under the 7th Pay Commission. Here is a full breakdown. | Pay Level: Level-11 under 7th CPC Basic Pay: ₹67,700/- per month Gross Emoluments (as of January 2026): Approximately ₹1,60,045/- per month (inclusive of DA and other applicable allowances) DA Revision: Remuneration will be revised in line with Central Government DA revision notifications from ESIC Headquarters. Private Practice: Strictly not permitted during the tenure. Tenure: 3 years (subject to satisfactory annual performance evaluation). No extension beyond 3 years.

selection_process: The ESIC Chennai Senior Resident 2026 selection is primarily interview-based
but there is an important provision for screening if footfall is high. Here is exactly how the process works.

Document Verification (9:00 AM – 11:00 AM): All candidates must report during this window. Only those whose documents pass verification will proceed to the interview. Screening Test (if applicable): If the candidate-to-post ratio exceeds 1:10

a screening test may be held to shortlist candidates at a 1:5 ratio. The test will consist of MCQs in the concerned discipline. Screening test marks do not carry weight in the final result. Walk-In Interview: Final selection is based solely on interview performance before the Selection Board. Result Publication: Results will be posted on the official website www.esic.gov.in/recruitments. Joining: Selected candidates must join immediately upon receiving the offer of engagement.

content_sections.heading: Official Notification – ESIC MCH KK Nagar Chennai Senior Resident 2026
content_sections.text: ESIC Medical College & Hospital, K.K. Nagar - a ISO 9001:2015 certified central government institution under the Employees' State Insurance Corporation (ESIC), Ministry of Labour & Employment, Government of India - proposes to fill 60 Senior Resident vacancies on a contractual basis across clinical, surgical, para-clinical, and super-specialty departments. The engagement is governed by the 7th Central Pay Commission pay structure and is subject to the terms of the contract, annual performance review, and compliance with NMC norms. The recruitment is being conducted through a direct walk-in interview - meaning eligible candidates can appear on the scheduled date without a prior written application submission to the office (though an advance email copy is recommended). Reservation rules follow Government of India guidelines , and 17 posts are horizontally reserved for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD) in identified posts. [PDF Download Link] – Click Here [Apply Online] – Email advance application copy to: [email protected] | For queries: [email protected]

content_sections.heading: Everything You Need to Know About ESIC MCH Chennai SR Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: If you are a postgraduate medical professional searching for a Senior Resident position at a central government hospital in Chennai, your opportunity has arrived. ESIC Medical College & Hospital, K.K. Nagar, Chennai has officially announced the engagement of 60 Senior Residents on a 3-year contractual tenure under the 7th Pay Commission through a walk-in interview scheduled on July 8 and 9, 2026 . This recruitment is open across 23 departments - ranging from Radiology and ICU to Forensic Medicine and Medical Oncology - covering both clinical and non-clinical specialties. Candidates from reserved categories (OBC, SC, ST, EWS) and persons with disabilities (PwD) will find dedicated vacancies under this drive

content_sections.heading: ESIC Senior Resident Recruitment 2026 - Total Vacancies
content_sections.text: Additionally, 17 out of 60 posts are horizontally reserved for Persons with Disabilities (PwD) in identified specialties.

content_sections.heading: Walk-In Interview Day 1 – July 8, 2026 (Wednesday)
content_sections.text: The first day of the ESIC KK Nagar Chennai walk-in interview covers predominantly non-clinical and allied specialties. Here is the full vacancy breakdown for departments scheduled on Day 1.

content_sections.heading: Walk-In Interview Day 2 – July 9, 2026 (Thursday)
content_sections.text: Day 2 of the ESIC Chennai Senior Resident walk-in interview focuses on major clinical and super-specialty departments. This is the larger pool of vacancies, covering high-demand fields like ICU, Emergency Medicine, and Cardiology.

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria – Who Can Apply?
content_sections.text: Understanding the eligibility requirements is critical before appearing for the ESIC Senior Resident walk-in interview 2026. Below are the academic qualifications, age limits, and departmental preferences as per NMC-TEQ 2025 and PGMER 2023 norms. Clinical specialties (broad specialties): Upper age limit - 45 years Pre-clinical / Para-clinical subjects (Anatomy, Biochemistry, Physiology, Pharmacology, Microbiology, Pathology, Forensic Medicine, Community Medicine): Upper age limit - 50 years A candidate must hold one of the following: MD / MS / DNB (Postgraduate degree in Doctor of Medicine, Master of Surgery, or Diplomate of National Board), or M.Sc. with Ph.D. in medical subjects - Anatomy, Biochemistry, Physiology, Pharmacology, or Microbiology All candidates must satisfy eligibility as per NMC Teachers Eligibility Qualifications (TEQ) 2025 read with MSR/PGMER 2023 as on the date of interview. This is a critical section for candidates - check your department's preferred order before appearing at the ESIC Chennai walk-in interview. Two departments allow candidates from related specialties to also apply - this significantly widens eligibility for those departments: Emergency Medicine: Candidates holding MD/MS/DNB in General Medicine, Anaesthesia, Respiratory Medicine, General Surgery, or Orthopaedics can appear - in addition to their own specialty. Physical Medicine & Rehabilitation: Candidates with MD (General Medicine) + Diploma in PMR, or MS/DNB in General Surgery or Orthopaedics are also eligible.

rows.preferential_order_2: MD/MS/DNB - General Medicine, Anaesthesia, Respiratory Medicine, General Surgery, Orthopaedics
MS/MD/DNB - Medicine with Diploma in PMR, General Surgery, Orthopaedics
MD/DNB – General Medicine / Paediatrics / Respiratory Medicine

content_sections.heading: Salary & Remuneration – 7th CPC Pay Scale
content_sections.text: One of the most sought-after aspects of ESIC Senior Resident recruitment is its competitive pay structure under the 7th Pay Commission. Here is a full breakdown. Pay Level: Level-11 under 7th CPC Basic Pay: ₹67,700/- per month Gross Emoluments (as of January 2026): Approximately ₹1,60,045/- per month (inclusive of DA and other applicable allowances) DA Revision: Remuneration will be revised in line with Central Government DA revision notifications from ESIC Headquarters. Private Practice: Strictly not permitted during the tenure. Tenure: 3 years (subject to satisfactory annual performance evaluation). No extension beyond 3 years.

content_sections.heading: Reservation Policy
content_sections.text: ESIC Chennai Senior Resident recruitment follows Central Government of India reservation guidelines. Here is what each category needs to know. OBC (Non-Creamy Layer): Must submit community certificate in the prescribed Central Government format (DoPT OM No. 36036/2/2013-Estt(Res) dated 30.05.2014). Certificates in other formats will not be accepted. SC / ST: Must hold a valid caste/tribe certificate issued by an authorized government authority. EWS: Must possess a valid income & assets certificate showing gross annual family income below ₹8 lakh and no disqualifying property assets. PwD (Persons with Disability): 17 posts horizontally reserved. Minimum 40% disability required. Certificate must be issued by a government-constituted Medical Board only - certificates from individual hospitals or practitioners are not valid. Ex-Servicemen: Fee exemption applicable; discharge summary required.

content_sections.heading: Application Fee
content_sections.text: The ESIC Senior Resident 2026 walk-in interview requires payment of a non-refundable application fee for most categories. Here is a clear breakdown. Mode of Payment: Demand Draft only, drawn in favour of "ESI Fund Account No. 1" , payable at Chennai, on any scheduled bank. The DD must be issued (i.e., after 17.06.2026). Cash, UPI, or online transfer are not accepted.

rows.category: SC / ST / PwD / Women Candidates / Ex-Servicemen

content_sections.heading: Documents Required at the Walk-In Interview
content_sections.text: All candidates must arrive between 9:00 AM and 11:00 AM with originals and one set of self-attested photocopies of the following documents. Document verification closes at 11:00 AM sharp - latecomers will not be entertained. Valid Photo ID (Aadhaar, PAN, Passport, Voter ID, or Driving Licence) Address Proof (Ration Card, Passport, Aadhaar, or Driving Licence) Class 10th certificate (for date of birth proof) Class 10th and 12th marksheets MBBS degree and marksheets MD / MS / DNB / DM / MCh degree and certificates Experience certificates (if applicable, as per NMC guidelines) Valid registration certificate from National Medical Commission (NMC) or State Medical Council Two recent passport-size photographs Community certificate (OBC / SC / ST / EWS) in the format prescribed for Central Government posts PwD disability certificate (from authorized Medical Board only) Ex-Serviceman discharge certificate (if applicable) NOC from current employer (if currently working in a government institution) Demand Draft for ₹500/- (if applicable) Filled application form in the prescribed Annexure C format (available in the official notification)

content_sections.heading: Selection Process – How Candidates Are Chosen
content_sections.text: The ESIC Chennai Senior Resident 2026 selection is primarily interview-based, but there is an important provision for screening if footfall is high. Here is exactly how the process works. Document Verification (9:00 AM – 11:00 AM): All candidates must report during this window. Only those whose documents pass verification will proceed to the interview. Screening Test (if applicable): If the candidate-to-post ratio exceeds 1:10 , a screening test may be held to shortlist candidates at a 1:5 ratio . The test will consist of MCQs in the concerned discipline. Screening test marks do not carry weight in the final result. Walk-In Interview: Final selection is based solely on interview performance before the Selection Board. Result Publication: Results will be posted on the official website www.esic.gov.in/recruitments . Joining: Selected candidates must join immediately upon receiving the offer of engagement .

content_sections.heading: Important Terms & Conditions
content_sections.text: Before walking in, candidates should be fully aware of the contractual obligations and institutional rules governing this ESIC Senior Resident engagement. Candidates who have already completed 3 years under the Central Residency Scheme are not eligible . Government employees applying must carry a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from their employer. A Security Deposit of ₹30,000/- (Demand Draft in favour of ESI Fund A/C No. 1, payable at State Bank of India, Ashok Nagar, Chennai-78) is mandatory at the time of joining. This is forfeited if the candidate resigns or is terminated within 1 year. The contract may be terminated by either side with one month's notice or equivalent pay. Attendance must be marked both manually and via Aadhaar-enabled biometric (AEBAS) daily. Absence for 15 consecutive days without permission leads to automatic termination and forfeiture of security deposit. No PF, Pension, Medical Benefits, Seniority, or Promotion benefits are admissible under this contractual engagement. Registration with the Tamil Nadu Medical Council must be completed within 30 days of joining if not already registered. Candidates may apply to only one post at a time , except for Emergency Medicine and PMR where cross-specialty applications are permitted. A separate application form and DD are required for each post. A contractual agreement on a ₹100/- stamp paper must be signed at the time of joining.

content_sections.heading: Important Dates & Contact Information
content_sections.text: Staying updated is crucial since any change in interview dates will be announced on the official website only. Candidates are advised to check regularly.

faq.question: Q1. What is the total number of vacancies in ESIC Chennai Senior Resident Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: ESIC Medical College & Hospital, K.K. Nagar, Chennai has announced 60 Senior Resident posts (3-year tenure under 7th CPC) across 23 departments - 19 posts on July 8 and 41 posts on July 9, 2026.

faq.question: Q2. When and where is the ESIC Chennai walk-in interview 2026?
faq.answer: The walk-in interview is scheduled on July 8, 2026 (Wednesday) and July 9, 2026 (Thursday) at ESIC Medical College & Hospital, Ashok Pillar Road, K.K. Nagar, Chennai – 600 078. Candidates must report between 9:00 AM and 11:00 AM for document verification.

faq.question: Q3. What is the salary for ESIC Senior Resident posts under 7th CPC?
faq.answer: The basic pay is ₹67,700/- per month at Pay Level-11 under the 7th Pay Commission. The gross monthly emoluments (as of January 2026) are approximately ₹1,60,045/-, inclusive of applicable allowances.

faq.question: Q4. Who is eligible to apply for ESIC KK Nagar Senior Resident posts?
faq.answer: Candidates holding MD / MS / DNB in the relevant specialty, or M.Sc. with Ph.D. in applicable pre-clinical subjects are eligible. Age limit is 45 years for clinical specialties and 50 years for pre-clinical departments. Eligibility must conform to NMC-TEQ 2025 and PGMER 2023 standards.

faq.question: Q5. Is there any written exam for ESIC Senior Resident recruitment in Chennai?
faq.answer: Normally, selection is based on walk-in interview performance. However, if applicants exceed 10 times the number of vacancies per post, the authorities may conduct a screening MCQ test to shortlist candidates at a 1:5 ratio. Screening test marks do not affect the final selection.

faq.question: Q6. What is the application fee for ESIC Senior Resident 2026?
faq.answer: The fee is ₹500/- for UR, OBC, and EWS candidates, payable by Demand Draft only in favour of "ESI Fund Account No. 1" payable at Chennai. SC, ST, PwD, women, and ex-servicemen are exempt from the fee.

faq.question: Q7. Can a doctor with a General Medicine degree apply for ICU or Emergency Medicine at ESIC Chennai?
faq.answer: Yes. For ICU, candidates with MD/DNB in General Medicine are eligible as Preferential Order 2. For Emergency Medicine, candidates with MD/DNB/MS in General Medicine, Anaesthesia, Respiratory Medicine, General Surgery, or Orthopaedics are eligible in addition to Emergency Medicine specialists.

faq.question: Q8. How many posts are reserved for PwD candidates in ESIC Senior Resident 2026?
faq.answer: 17 out of 60 posts are horizontally reserved for Persons with Benchmark Disability (PwBD) in identified specialties. A minimum of 40% disability, certified by a duly constituted government Medical Board, is required.

faq.question: Q9. Is private practice allowed during the ESIC Senior Resident tenure?
faq.answer: No. Private practice of any kind is strictly prohibited during the 3-year contractual engagement with ESIC.

faq.question: Q10. Where can I download the official notification and application form?
faq.answer: The official PDF notification including the application form (Annexure C) is available on the ESIC official recruitment portal.
